$comment: SMGP team-season history capsules, season 10 (SMGP-024). Base-universe history: the world's own arc, NOT the player career. DISPLAY-ONLY, never a fold input.

team.madonna.summary: The house that never explains itself spent the winter making the PALM 190 V10 survive the detune, and emerged with the same answer as always: the MADONNA 456 posted the reference time of the new formula before anyone else had read it twice. Senna treated the Charter as one more rulebook to bend, Asselin resumed the door, and the yellow-and-red garage moved through the booing spring in surgical calm. The only genuine engineering quarrel all year was braking stability into the new slow corners, settled by mid-season. The mood was serenity. The rivals were, as ever, everyone.
team.madonna.objective: Prove the pared-back formula changes nothing about who sets the reference.
team.madonna.definingEvent: Senna posts the winter's first Gen-4B reference time and goes home without a word.
team.madonna.carryForward: The benchmark enters the tyre-ration year with the whole grid still measuring itself against the #1.

team.firenze.summary: Second to the crown and to no one else was the creed, and the Charter year tested the first half of it hardest. The FIRENZE 500's detuned FIRENZE 99 V12 surrendered its straight-line opera, and the wine-dark house had to rediscover the corners, where Elssler's ice was always the sharper instrument. Germi gave the tifosi their theatre anyway, every apex a brushstroke, while the #3 banked the cold arithmetic the new law rewards. The winter's triumph was a tyre-temperature package the whole paddock envied; its failure, a brake balance that fought the drivers until spring. Above them, as ever, Madonna.
team.firenze.objective: Turn the craft formula into the second interruption the dynasty has owed Firenze for years.
team.firenze.definingEvent: Elssler walks the re-profiled walls all winter with the Commission, then races what he helped write.
team.firenze.carryForward: The ice enters the allocation era convinced the next rulebook was written in his own handwriting.

team.millions.summary: The gold freight met a law it could not outbid. A fifth of the DICK MD V10's fury was pared away by signature rather than invoice, and the MILLIONS 189 spent the spring as the loudest of the muscle cars relearning its trade. Jones grumbled through the re-education at full volume while Alberti took his other seat, the one at the Commission table, walking the re-profiled walls beside Moreau and lending the new law the establishment's own voice. The garage's best winter work was a fuel-thrift package; its private embarrassment was discovering that money buys no exemption from physics.
team.millions.objective: Keep the chrome garage at the front of a formula its fortune cannot buy back.
team.millions.definingEvent: Alberti walks every rebuilt barrier on the calendar as the Commission's driver voice.
team.millions.carryForward: A garage built to out-spend everyone must now learn to out-think, with a tyre ration coming.

team.bestowal.summary: No house took the Charter's power cut more personally. Bestowal's whole cathedral had been raised on the VAPOR DN's brutal blessing, and the Gen-4B template taxed away exactly the advantage the benefactor's patience had purchased. Blume answered with metronomic correctness and heirloom silences; Picos kept converting Sundays with warm precision where the German steel no longer overwhelmed. The winter's salvage was a braking package that made the BESTOWAL 167 the grid's surest stopper into the new slow corners. In the marble garage the mood was offense worn as discipline, and the resentment had a name: the rule-makers.
team.bestowal.objective: Prove German steel still matters in a formula written to humble it.
team.bestowal.definingEvent: The first Gen-4B dyno sheet lands in the marble garage and the room goes very quiet.
team.bestowal.carryForward: The benefactor's patience is now aimed at craft, and the cathedral wants its altitude back.

team.blanche.summary: The metronome teams discovered the new rules had practically been drafted in their image, and none more than the whitecoats. The BLANCHE 582, never the most powerful thing on the grid, lost least to the detune, and Herbin spent the season parked in mirrors the white car had never darkened before. The DELTA 103 V10's thrift became a weapon rather than a compromise; White supplied the warmth and the odd brave afternoon. The irritation was new: the best-of-the-rest argument suddenly had company, Hamano and Pacheco climbing, and Blanche did not enjoy sharing.
team.blanche.objective: Convert a rulebook written for the white car into the best-of-the-rest crown, uncontested.
team.blanche.definingEvent: Herbin heads the first compressed-field qualifying sheet the white garage has ever topped.
team.blanche.carryForward: The white car expects the tyre ration to suit it, and expects the company to keep coming.

team.tyrant.summary: The gunmetal boot camp had to learn a discipline it used to sneer at, and took to it fast. Hamano's forty-lap patience was suddenly the sharpest weapon in the midfield, the #11 banking the steadiest afternoons of the house's life and climbing into the best-of-the-rest argument. The season's soul belonged to the #12: Gould came back from the Australia fright at Canada, along the wall that bred him, to the loudest reception of the year. The TYRANT 548's LIZZIE 24 V8 met the detune with obstinate reliability. The crew stopped sneering when they saw what it paid.
team.tyrant.objective: Trade intimidation for arithmetic without losing the menace.
team.tyrant.definingEvent: Gould returns at Canada and the island gives him the season's loudest welcome.
team.tyrant.carryForward: Two drivers, one blade each: the crossover patience and the island nerve, both sharpened by law.

team.losel.summary: The slide-rule garage had waited five seasons for someone to outlaw brawling, and the Charter obliged. With muscle regulated down, the LOSEL 125's virtues, braking stability, tyre life, traffic craft, became the whole examination, and Pacheco sat it like a man who had written the paper. Positions the horsepower years always stole back started staying banked, and the #13 climbed into the argument at the front of LEVEL B. Dehehe's blue metronome ticked in support. The one live file was the VAPOR DNPQ V8's thirst, a fuel curve the engineers spent all autumn filing down.
team.losel.objective: Prove that when the sport becomes calculation, Losel becomes the favorite.
team.losel.definingEvent: Pacheco's first front-of-midfield qualifying run under Charter power validates a decade of data.
team.losel.carryForward: A one-allocation tyre rule is coming, and the data door considers it a home fixture.

team.may.summary: After three seasons of counting coins against the horsepower merchants, the leaf-green garage finally met a rulebook that cost less to obey. The MAY 555 was never powerful, so the detune barely touched it, and Turner spent the year mugging richer machines in the compressed field and waving at the grandstands. The LORRY 32 V8, old, rebuilt and loved, finished nearly everything it started. Spring's optimism, for once, looked less like faith and more like arithmetic. The one grey morning was realizing how many other small garages had been handed the same gift.
team.may.objective: Turn Charter parity into the points the horsepower years kept pricing out of reach.
team.may.definingEvent: Turner mugs two aristocrats in one compressed-field afternoon and waves at the grandstand both times.
team.may.carryForward: The little teams' gift is now shared property, and May must find an edge hope alone cannot buy.

team.bullets.summary: The insurgent watched the grandees surrender a fifth of their fury and said what everyone was thinking: the cut levels nothing for a garage that never had the power to lose. Freed from chasing horsepower it could never afford, the backstreet crew aimed the featherweight BULLETS 560 at the new craft examination, and Ceara spent the season reminding the aristocrats that hands were always his currency. The hand-fettled LIZZIE 24 V8 gave up its smoke and kept its temper. The compressed field suited the loaded gun: fewer straights to lose, more corners to steal.
team.bullets.objective: Make the craft era admit what the horsepower era never would: the #17 belongs at the front.
team.bullets.definingEvent: Ceara tells the press the power cut changes nothing for him, and the stopwatch starts agreeing.
team.bullets.carryForward: The insurgent has smelled parity at last, and the old fire begins, quietly, to draw breath.

team.dardan.summary: The oxblood garage arrived with its old war starved of fuel. The tribunal of two summers past had left Bellini diminished and the sister-chassis grudge looking like an old man's argument, and the Charter's craft examination asked questions the spear was not forged to answer. Alfven, ice over oxblood, became the season's quiet lesson: still running, clean, when the fights wrecked themselves ahead of him. The winter's real gain was a brake-cooling package; the unspoken loss was what the detune did to the VAPOR DNPQ V8's favourite weapon, intimidation by arrival. The clan brooded, sharpening a different edge.
team.dardan.objective: Re-forge the spear for a formula that punishes the charge and rewards the patient.
team.dardan.definingEvent: Alfven, not Bellini, banks the house's best afternoon of the Charter spring.
team.dardan.carryForward: The grudge against the sister garage goes quiet, and quiet, at Dardan, is never peace.

team.linden.summary: The oldest number on the grid drove the safest season of his long life and called it his proudest. Moreau had spent the winter walking every circuit for the Commission, tape and notebook in hand, and the reward was a calendar he had helped make survivable, raced with the usual unhurried green patience. The LINDEN LN198 was never fast, and was now, gloriously, not alone in that: the compressed field let the patient root bank harvests the horsepower years denied him. The LORRY 32 V8 finished, as it always finishes. The paddock set its watch by the old tree.
team.linden.objective: Race the law he helped write, and prove endurance is its own kind of crown.
team.linden.definingEvent: Moreau drives the Charter's first season and declares it, simply, his proudest.
team.linden.carryForward: The elder statesman of the whole paddock now has a legacy louder than any result sheet.

team.minarae.summary: The forge's two hands could not have been further apart. Miller ground out the old canyon craft, hunting top tens with a car that answered patience, while Nono spent the Charter's first year fighting the new law with old violence, and the scenery won more often than he did. The MINARAE 594 was honest enough; the SEGA SG1000 V8 gave what it had. Inside the garage the old argument rose nearer the surface: the talent was real, the finishes were not. Miller watched, graded his heir lap by lap, and kept his counsel one season more.
team.minarae.objective: Turn the loudest promise in LEVEL C into the finishes the garage keeps promising everyone.
team.minarae.definingEvent: Nono throws another points afternoon at the scenery, and the garage goes very quiet at him.
team.minarae.carryForward: The reckoning between the heir and the workshop is coming, and everyone at Minarae knows it.

team.rigel.summary: The half-museum had a quietly decent year, which at Rigel passes for a golden age. The detune touched the old restoration nothing, the starlight cars had been slow for a decade and were suddenly merely slower, and the compressed field let Cotman and Chardin finish far more often than the horsepower years permitted. The RIGEL 3000's LORRY 32 V8, coaxed along on memory and stubbornness, became the pit lane's favourite small mercy: old bloodline, still crossing lines. Cotman counted his finishes like birthdays; Chardin held doors with a courtly hand. The mechanics allowed themselves one evening of looking forward.
team.rigel.objective: Restore the season, one finish at a time, until the light is worth catching again.
team.rigel.definingEvent: Both midnight cars cross the line in the same race for the first time in years.
team.rigel.carryForward: The museum believes, faintly and privately, that the new era might have room for restorers.

team.comet.summary: Down where hope is a maintenance schedule, the Charter year was another season of chasing the old light. Tornio did what Tornio does: no interviews, no theatre, the right foot doing all the talking, racing the smoke rather than the crown and beating it to the flag more often than usual. The COMET 323's LIZZIE 24 V8 profited from everyone else's austerity, and the black car reached the finish with a regularity the garage chalked up as its quiet triumph. The veterans of The Season agreed this had not been it either, and rolled the car inside to wait.
team.comet.objective: Keep the machine alive, the faith maintained, and the old highlight reel unembarrassed.
team.comet.definingEvent: Tornio strings together the longest finishing run the garage has logged since the comet year.
team.comet.carryForward: The pan is still cold, but the romantics in the garage have stopped apologizing for hoping.

team.orchis.summary: The hothouse bloom found the Charter's gentler speeds marginally more hospitable, though the tragedy held. Given cool air and a smooth circuit the ORCHIS 056 sang; the shove of the compressed pack made it wilt. Tegner watered, waited and watched the sky, and on the grey afternoons the pale car stole respectable finishes from garages that cost ten times more. The MISFIRE 50 V8, detuned like everything else, at least stopped expiring in the heat. The gardener's ledgers recorded the finest finishing rate in years, and one frost-gambling Saturday that nearly, nearly came off.
team.orchis.objective: Find the handful of afternoons where the conditions forgive the flower, and be there for all of them.
team.orchis.definingEvent: Tegner's frost gamble on a grey Saturday falls one lap short of the steal of the season.
team.orchis.carryForward: The glasshouse has learned the new era has cool hours in it, and intends to own them all.

team.zeroforce.summary: The end of the road kept its pit box. The Charter compressed the field but did not conjure budget, and the ZEROFORCE 231 spent too many Sundays parked on the grass while Klinger counted laps the way other men count winnings. The mercy of the new era was arithmetic: the old LORRY 32 V8, nursed rather than thrashed, reached the flag more often than in the horsepower years, and each finish went on the wall in chalk. Gallows humour held. The refusal to be classified dead last held. Nothing else changed; the crew counted that a victory.
team.zeroforce.objective: Keep the lights on, keep the car circulating, and refuse the foot of the classification one more year.
team.zeroforce.definingEvent: The #32 reaches the flag three times running, a streak the garage records in chalk.
team.zeroforce.carryForward: The floor hears a rumour the next formula will sell engines that finish, and dares not hope aloud.

team.joke.summary: The carnival crew adored the new formula instantly, because a compressed field is a sandbagger's paradise. Dufay dawdled through Saturdays a second off the pace, let the big board scratch the #16 off entirely, and then arrived at hairpins on Sundays with the throttle buried, mugging cars that had ignored him all weekend. The JOKE 777's POND V8 was no jewel and needed none; close racing is the greasepaint's natural habitat. The ledger stayed in the middle of LEVEL B, and the paddock's favourite punchline banked enough to keep the establishment's door under grinning siege.
team.joke.objective: Turn the era of close racing into the longest-running practical joke in the paddock.
team.joke.definingEvent: Dufay sandbags all Saturday, then mugs three midfield cars in one Sunday stint.
team.joke.carryForward: The establishment has stopped scratching the #16 off on Saturday night, which spoils half the fun.

team.lares.summary: The hearth-red house kept the lamp lit and the finishes coming, which is the whole creed. Arai's courteous patience suited the Charter's craft examination from its first afternoon, and the LARES 92, never quick through the traps, was suddenly among the surest things on the grid to still be circulating at the flag. The RAM V12's appetite remained the oldest quarrel, fuel figures fought all year for modest gains. The mood was uncommonly warm: the mantel had room for more, and the men who build reliability the way others build speed suspected their season was coming.
team.lares.objective: Prove the house's whole philosophy, that a car which comes home is its own result.
team.lares.definingEvent: Arai brings the hearth-red car home in every one of the season's first ten rounds.
team.lares.carryForward: The little garage senses a rulebook coming that will make patience the whole sport, and it is ready.

team.feet.summary: The working man's team simply out-worked the new formula. Where richer garages mourned their lost horses, the FEET 13 had never had any to lose, and Rampal dragged the car past machinery worth ten of it, week after week. The YOUGEN V10, hand-fettled and honest, detuned gracefully and finished stoutly. The compressed field meant Old Boots came home in the points more often, each one earned down to the final bolt. Up and down the pit lane the mechanics tipped their caps. The grandstand did not chant the name; the garage did not care.
team.feet.objective: Earn everything, shortcut nothing, and make the Charter's compressed field pay honest wages.
team.feet.definingEvent: Rampal drags the #24 past a works car on merit, and the whole pit lane sees it.
team.feet.carryForward: The grafters have proof the new era pays for sweat, and intend to invoice it weekly.

team.serga.summary: The hand-built long shot entered the Charter era still wearing the thousand small names the whip-round bought it, and the grey car's whole season read like a thank-you letter at racing speed. Sambena welded, drove and signed his own cheques; the SERGA 1000's SC3000 F12 detuned without complaint and, relieved of the horsepower chase, reached the flag with a regularity he chalked up as his kind of championship. The compressed field let the #25 trouble the lower midfield. Courteous to a fault, he yielded to no one when it mattered, and the pit lane loved him for it.
team.serga.objective: Keep the hand-built car on the grid, on merit, and honour every small name on its flanks.
team.serga.definingEvent: The #25 finishes the Charter's first season entire, sixteen rounds, all of them paid back.
team.serga.carryForward: The garage that was saved by the paddock now stands as proof of what the paddock is for.

team.cool.summary: The calmest garage in racing decided the Safety Charter was a fine reason to relax. While the paddock howled and argued, the deckchairs came out, the sunglasses went on, and Delvaux chased the light around the compressed field. The COOL 05's CORSE V8 was always more sunset than weapon, and the detune changed nothing the garage cared about. A top ten remained a festival, a finish a fine afternoon, and both came more often now. The big board said what it liked; Cool read it over a cold drink and raised a glass to the medical corps.
team.cool.objective: Turn up, go racing, enjoy the ride, and let the panicked paddock wear itself out.
team.cool.definingEvent: Delvaux banks a top ten and the garage declares a festival before the car is even back.
team.cool.carryForward: The deckchairs have survived the horsepower war; nothing the new era proposes can frighten them.

team.moon.summary: The far side of the grid kept circling, and found the new era marginally less lonely. The MOON 292's RAM V12, rebuilt once more on fumes and faith, lasted longer against the detuned pack than it ever had against full horsepower, and Yepes waved the leaders through with the unkillable grin and banked finishes that would once have been miracles. The silver car remained distant, cold and faithful. No one pretended the Charter had changed the address, but the garage noted, in low voices, that the gap to the pack had measurably narrowed, and treated the measurement like moonrise.
team.moon.objective: Keep circling, keep the lights on, and shorten the distance to the pack by one honest measure.
team.moon.definingEvent: The #30 finishes within sight of the midfield for the first time anyone can remember.
team.moon.carryForward: The telescope stays pointed up; the orbit, for once, is genuinely tightening.

team.iris.summary: The white-glove house took the craft formula as a compliment and the results as an insult. The IRIS 717 was built for this examination, jewel-cut balance, immaculate stops, nothing wasted, and Salgado spent the season throwing the violet car at the gap the pared formula was supposed to close, grinning when it refused. The PRISM 90 V10 detuned cleanly, the gloves stayed unsmudged, and the form book kept the fire in the contender column all year. The cathedral's mood was hunger worn as patience: the crown he lifted once was still up there, and the fire had not cooled.
team.iris.objective: Turn the most precise car on the grid into the second crown the violet flame keeps promising.
team.iris.definingEvent: Salgado splits the grandees at the Charter's first restart and the cathedral allows itself to believe.
team.iris.carryForward: The flame wants the hunt formalized, and the white gloves are drawing up the campaign.

team.azalea.summary: The frost found her weather. Charter parity rewarded everything the glasshouse had cultivated for a decade, patience, poise, the held breath, and Larssen began appearing on form-book pages that had never carried her name. The AZALEA 808 gave up little to the detune and gained everything from the compression, the BLOOM 88 V8's manners finally worth more than raw anger. The one internal quarrel was strategic: when to spend the bloom and when to keep it budded. By autumn the big board began, cautiously, to reprice the #34. The sister house across the shared bay noticed, and filed it away.
team.azalea.objective: Convert a decade of patience into the form-book presence the frost has always promised.
team.azalea.definingEvent: Larssen climbs to a career-best grid slot under Charter parity, and the frost is noticed.
team.azalea.carryForward: The glasshouse knows now that its weather exists, and starts planning whole seasons around it.
